previous_statement: N1.4 (LIMIT PASSAGE in operator norm). R_F j = j R_Phi, where R_F := (S_F^2)^{-1/2} = lim_N g_N(S_F^2) in A and R_Phi := (S_Phi^2)^{-1/2} = lim_N g_N(S_Phi^2) in B (def-spectral-idempotent). DERIVATION. (i) CONVERGENCE: by GT-taylor-calculus with f(x)=x^{-1/2}, x0=1, applied to X=S_F^2 in A (||S_F^2 - I_A|| = 4||F^2-F|| < 1 by GT-hyp H2, inside radius), the series f(X) is well-defined, i.e. the partial sums g_N(S_F^2) converge IN THE BANACH-ALGEBRA ELEMENT NORM OF A (the norm in which A is a Banach algebra, GT-taylor-calculus) to R_F; identically g_N(S_Phi^2) -> R_Phi in B's element norm (||S_Phi^2 - I_B|| = 4||Phi^2-Phi|| < 1). The Taylor tail bound ||g_N(X)-f(X)|| <= sum_{n>N} |a_n| ||X - x0 I||^n -> 0 (a convergent tail of sum |a_n| t^n, t=||X-x0 I||<1) makes the convergence quantitative: set eps_N^F := ||R_F - g_N(S_F^2)|| -> 0 and eps_N^Phi := ||R_Phi - g_N(S_Phi^2)|| -> 0 (element norms in A, B). (ii) ESTIMATE: for every N, R_F j - j R_Phi = (R_F - g_N(S_F^2)) j + (g_N(S_F^2) j - j g_N(S_Phi^2)) + j (g_N(S_Phi^2) - R_Phi). The MIDDLE term vanishes for every N by node 1.3. So R_F j - j R_Phi = (R_F - g_N(S_F^2)) j + j (g_N(S_Phi^2) - R_Phi). Taking the (operator) norm of these maps B->A and the triangle inequality: ||R_F j - j R_Phi||_op <= ||(R_F - g_N(S_F^2)) j||_op + ||j (g_N(S_Phi^2) - R_Phi)||_op. Now (R_F - g_N(S_F^2)) is an ELEMENT a of the Banach algebra A and 'a j' is left-multiplication by a composed with j; the bound is BANACH-ALGEBRA SUBMULTIPLICATIVITY of A's element norm (GT-hyp H1) together with j bounded (H3): for ||y||<=1, ||(a j)(y)|| = ||a . j(y)|| <= ||a|| ||j(y)|| (H1 submultiplicativity, ||a x||<=||a|| ||x|| in A) <= ||a|| ||j||_op, so ||a j||_op <= ||a|| ||j||_op = ||R_F - g_N(S_F^2)|| ||j||_op; symmetrically, with U := g_N(S_Phi^2) - R_Phi an element of B, ||j U||_op <= ||j||_op ||U|| since ||(j U)(y)|| = ||j(U . y)|| <= ||j||_op ||U . y|| <= ||j||_op ||U|| ||y|| (H3 then H1 submultiplicativity in B). Hence ||R_F j - j R_Phi||_op <= ||R_F - g_N(S_F^2)|| ||j||_op + ||j||_op ||g_N(S_Phi^2) - R_Phi|| = ||j||_op (eps_N^F + eps_N^Phi). [These two bounds use the BANACH-ALGEBRA NORM AXIOM (H1 submultiplicativity ||a x||<=||a|| ||x||) plus j bounded (H3), NOT the operator-composition fact for two endomorphisms; a, U are algebra elements with their element norms, not endomorphisms of A->A.] (iii) LIMIT: the left side ||R_F j - j R_Phi||_op is a FIXED nonnegative number independent of N, while the right side ||j||_op (eps_N^F + eps_N^Phi) -> 0 as N->infinity (||j||_op < infinity finite by GT-hyp H3; eps_N -> 0 by (i)). A fixed number <= a sequence tending to 0 must be <= 0, hence = 0. Therefore R_F j = j R_Phi. Uses node 1.3, GT-taylor-calculus, GT-hyp, def-spectral-idempotent.
new_statement: N1.4 (LIMIT PASSAGE in operator norm). R_F j = j R_Phi, where R_F := (S_F^2)^{-1/2} = lim_N g_N(S_F^2) in A and R_Phi := (S_Phi^2)^{-1/2} = lim_N g_N(S_Phi^2) in B (def-spectral-idempotent). DERIVATION. (i) CONVERGENCE: by GT-taylor-calculus with f(x)=x^{-1/2}, x0=1, applied to X=S_F^2 in A (||S_F^2 - I_A|| = 4||F^2-F|| < 1 by GT-hyp H2, inside radius), the series f(X) is well-defined, i.e. the partial sums g_N(S_F^2) converge IN THE BANACH-ALGEBRA ELEMENT NORM OF A (the norm in which A is a Banach algebra, GT-taylor-calculus) to R_F; identically g_N(S_Phi^2) -> R_Phi in B's element norm (||S_Phi^2 - I_B|| = 4||Phi^2-Phi|| < 1). The Taylor tail bound ||g_N(X)-f(X)|| <= sum_{n>N} |a_n| ||X - x0 I||^n -> 0 (a convergent TAIL of sum |a_n| t^n with t=||X-x0 I||<1; the absolute summability sum_n |a_n| t^n < infinity for t<1 is the standard absolute-convergence-strictly-inside-the-radius-of-convergence fact for the (-1/2)-binomial series, GT-binomial-series, a flagged standard-analysis gap matching the VALIDATED lem-P-properties) makes the convergence quantitative: set eps_N^F := ||R_F - g_N(S_F^2)|| -> 0 and eps_N^Phi := ||R_Phi - g_N(S_Phi^2)|| -> 0 (element norms in A, B). Hence ||R_F j - j R_Phi||_op <= ||R_F - g_N(S_F^2)|| ||j||_op + ||j||_op ||g_N(S_Phi^2) - R_Phi|| = ||j||_op (eps_N^F + eps_N^Phi). [These two bounds use the BANACH-ALGEBRA NORM AXIOM (H1 submultiplicativity ||a x||<=||a|| ||x||) plus j bounded (H3), NOT the operator-composition fact for two endomorphisms; a, U are algebra elements with their element norms, not endomorphisms of A->A.] (iii) LIMIT: the left side ||R_F j - j R_Phi||_op is a FIXED nonnegative number independent of N, while the right side ||j||_op (eps_N^F + eps_N^Phi) -> 0 as N->infinity (||j||_op < infinity finite by GT-hyp H3; eps_N -> 0 by (i)). A fixed number <= a sequence tending to 0 must be <= 0, hence = 0. Therefore R_F j = j R_Phi. Uses node 1.3, GT-taylor-calculus, GT-binomial-series, GT-hyp, def-spectral-idempotent.
